takeaways: Senior housing transaction volume hit $24B over the trailing four quarters — the highest since Q2 2015 — as occupancy pushes toward 90%+ and Welltower deploys $23B in deals focused on the sector.
Fed funds sit at 3.75–4.00% with the 10-year Treasury near 4.00–4.25%; Colliers projects 15–20% CRE sales volume growth in 2026, but lenders remain selective on marginal deals.
Agentic AI is shifting proptech from pilots to delivery — 92% of CRE firms now run AI pilots, with workflow integration replacing tool sprawl as the dominant 2026 theme.

items.headline: Welltower reports Q1 earnings April 28; FFO seen up 20% YoY on senior housing strength
items.summary: Welltower releases Q1 2026 results after market close April 28 with a call April 29. Analysts project FFO of $1.44/share, a 20% jump from $1.20 a year ago, driven by same-store revenue growth and rising occupancy in seniors housing. Full-year normalized FFO guidance: $6.09–$6.25.

items.headline: Welltower announces $23B in transactions, doubles down on senior housing
items.summary: Welltower disclosed roughly $23B of transactions tied to an intensified senior housing focus, including offloading an 18M SF medical office portfolio. The pivot signals a 'transformative' reallocation toward seniors housing for long-term growth — reshaping competitive dynamics for operators including Lloyd Jones.

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy hits 89.9% as transaction volume reaches $24B
items.summary: NIC MAP reports primary-market occupancy at 89.9% and secondary at 90% as of Q4 2025, marking 19 straight quarters of positive absorption. Rolling four-quarter transaction volume reached $24B at year-end 2025 — the highest since Q2 2015. Limited new supply should push average occupancy above 90% in 2026.

items.headline: Fed holds at 3.75–4.00% after October cut; no more 2026 cuts signaled
items.summary: The federal funds range sits at 3.75–4.00% following the Fed's second consecutive cut in late October. Powell indicated no further cuts are likely this year. The 10-year Treasury has stabilized in the 4.00–4.25% band, anchoring most fixed-rate CRE pricing.

items.headline: Commercial mortgage rates start at 5.30% in mid-April 2026
items.summary: Select Commercial reports CRE mortgage rates beginning at 5.30% as of April 20, with conventional commercial mortgages ranging 5.02%–8.75% by property type and credit. Borrowers with strong cash flow and conservative leverage continue to clear the market; marginal deals stall.

items.headline: Agentic AI named the next phase of proptech
items.summary: ICSC frames agentic AI as the defining 2026 shift, with autonomous agents automating procurement, pricing, inventory, leasing, and event programming across retail and CRE portfolios. Operators that wire agents into existing systems early will lock in operating-cost advantages.

items.headline: 92% of CRE firms now running AI pilots; market headed to $159.9B by 2033
items.summary: AI pilot adoption among commercial real estate companies has jumped from 5% to 92% in three years. The AI-in-proptech market is forecast to reach $159.9B by 2033, with the strongest near-term ROI in tenant relationship management, lease drafting, and portfolio analytics.

items.headline: 71% of leaders report higher stress; 40% considering leaving their roles
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership trends report finds 71% of leaders facing increased stress, with 40% weighing an exit. Meanwhile 77% of CHROs say they lack bench strength for critical roles. Talent risk and succession planning are now boardroom-level issues.

items.headline: Sonida closes $1.8B CNL deal, leans into labor optimization
items.summary: Sonida Senior Living completed its $1.8B CNL portfolio acquisition in March, immediately turning to labor cost optimization to preserve margins. The deal underscores how scale and operating leverage are becoming table stakes as M&A surges across the senior living sector.

items.headline: REIT ownership of senior housing draws scrutiny over resident care
items.summary: NPR reports REITs now own roughly one-fifth of US senior housing — including assisted living, memory care and independent living. Coverage raises concerns about whether financial-engineering priorities can erode care quality, a reputational risk operators must monitor.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in CRE sales volume for 2026
items.summary: Colliers projects a 15%–20% increase in commercial real estate sales volume this year as institutional and cross-border capital re-enters the market. Capital is flowing again, rates are easing modestly, and leasing fundamentals are stabilizing — a constructive backdrop for well-capitalized buyers.

items.headline: Multifamily deliveries down 75% YoY in Q1; rent growth set to accelerate
items.summary: Q1 2026 saw just 1,108 multifamily units delivered, 75% below the prior year, as new rental construction starts run 70% off peak. Combined with resilient demand, the supply pullback is expected to drive faster rent growth into 2026 — favorable for owners with stabilized assets.

items.headline: 'Workflow' becomes proptech's buzziest buzzword as tool sprawl loses patience
items.summary: Commercial Observer reports that real estate operators have less tolerance for isolated point tools and are pushing vendors to prove end-to-end workflow impact. The 2026 story is less about adopting more tech and more about making the existing stack actually work together.

items.headline: AI moves from experimentation to delivery in compliance, fraud, and document review
items.summary: Practical AI deployments in 2026 are concentrated in compliance monitoring, document processing, transaction security, and fraud prevention — including verifying documentation and securing fund transfers. Lower-risk, high-volume back-office tasks remain the easiest wins for senior housing operators.

items.headline: Human + AI leadership partnership defines the 2026 management agenda
items.summary: Leading firms are pivoting from generic AI adoption to strategic upskilling — using AI to amplify human judgment in predictive analytics, feedback loops, and decision support. HR and L&D leaders who frame AI as augmentation, not replacement, see better adoption and retention.

items.headline: Lean orgs and 'quiet cracking' shape 2026 workforce challenges
items.summary: Many organizations entered 2026 already lean after layoffs hit a 10-year US high. Combined with stalled internal mobility ('job hugging') and rising 'quiet cracking,' the result is fragile bench strength — and a premium on managers who can stabilize teams under pressure.

items.headline: 89% of employees say prioritizing wellbeing improves their performance
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life-Wellness finds 89% of employees perform better when wellbeing is prioritized — reframing wellness from perk to productivity lever. Senior living operators have an edge: their workforces respond strongly to embedded, on-site wellness programming.

items.headline: Personalized wellness goes mainstream via AI analytics and at-home biomarkers
items.summary: 2026 marks an inflection in personalized wellness, powered by AI-driven analytics, at-home biomarker testing, and wearables that go well beyond steps and sleep. The shift opens a meaningful programming opportunity for senior living communities seeking premium amenity differentiation.

items.headline: BayCom Corp names three new executives to drive next-phase growth
items.summary: BayCom Corp announced on April 13 that its board appointed three new executives to lead the company's next phase of growth strategy. The leadership refresh is one of several mid-April community-bank moves as regional institutions reposition for a stabilizing rate environment.

items.headline: 'Emotional fitness' emerges as a new health category for 2026
items.summary: Wellness trend reports introduce emotional fitness as a distinct 2026 category — pairing cognitive performance with emotional resilience. Expect leadership development and resident programming to converge around mindfulness, longevity, and community-based 'third places.'
